g.text: Per-FD State Array — conn_t Memory Layout (reactor.h)
Green border | HOT path — fits in L1 cache (< 4 cycles)
Blue border | Struct definition / array layout
Purple border | Array indexing — O(1) no pointer chase
Orange border | Heap allocations — cold, 1 miss per deref
Red border | Anti-pattern — hash map pointer chasing
0x08 | uint8_t* | read_buf // ptr to heap read buffer
0x10 | size_t | read_buf_len // bytes in read buffer
0x18 | uint8_t* | write_buf // ptr to heap write buffer
Total: 56 bytes per conn_t (padded to 64B cache line)
conn_t conn_table[MAX_CONNS] — Flat Array Indexed by fd
fd=2 | conn_table[2] | offset 0x000070 | CPU prefetch: sequential
Lookup: hash(fd) -> bucket -> linked list node
Step 2: node->next ptr chase — L2/L3 miss
Step 3: node->conn_t ptr chase — L3/DRAM miss (~200 cycles)
3 cache misses vs 1 cache miss for flat array
Flat array O(1): conn_table + fd * sizeof(conn_t)
No pointer chasing — single DRAM fetch, CPU prefetches next
Hot-Path Access Pattern per epoll_wait() Event
1. conn_t *c = conn_table + fd // 1 multiply + 1 add — no cache miss if hot
2. if c->state == FREE: skip // byte 0–3, already in cache line
3. if EPOLLIN: read into c->read_buf // byte 8–15, same cache line
4. update c->read_buf_len // byte 16–23, same cache line
5. if EPOLLOUT: flush c->write_buf // byte 24–39, same cache line
All 6 steps: ONE cache line loaded once — ~4 cycles after L1 hit
Byte 0–7 | fd + state | HOT — checked every event
Byte 8–15 | read_buf ptr | HOT — dereffed on EPOLLIN
Byte 24–31 | write_buf ptr | HOT — dereffed on EPOLLOUT
Byte 48–55 | timer_id | HOT — cancel on data recv
ALL 56 bytes fit in ONE 64-byte cache line
read_buf | malloc(4096) | COLD — only loaded on read
write_buf | malloc(65536) | COLD — only loaded on write
Pointer chase = 1 cache miss per dereference
Trade-off: struct stays in 1 cache line; data on demand
